mission.title: Install and Configure Kubeflow
mission.description: Kubeflow is a comprehensive machine learning toolkit for Kubernetes, designed to facilitate the entire AI lifecycle. It provides modular and scalable tools that can be used independently or as a complete AI reference platform. Key trade-offs include the complexity of setup and management versus the flexibility and power it offers for AI workloads.

steps.title: Install the Kubeflow CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the Kubeflow CLI, which is essential for managing your Kubeflow deployment.

```bash

curl -LO https://github.com/kubeflow/kubeflow/releases/download/v1.10.0/kubeflow-cli-linux-amd64.tar.gz

tar -xzf kubeflow-cli-linux-amd64.tar.gz

sudo mv kubeflow /usr/local/bin/

```

> Download the Kubeflow CLI tarball.
> Extract the CLI from the tarball.
> Move the CLI to a directory in your PATH.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a new Kubernetes cluster where Kubeflow will be deployed.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create kubeflow-cluster --cluster-version=1.30 --num-nodes=3 --region=us-central1

```

> Create a GKE cluster with 3 nodes.

steps.title: Set up kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures your kubeconfig to use the newly created cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials kubeflow-cluster --region=us-central1

```

> Fetch the cluster credentials.

steps.title: Verify the cluster nodes
steps.description:
This step checks that the nodes in the cluster are up and running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> List all nodes in the cluster.

steps.title: Install Kubeflow
steps.description:
This step deploys Kubeflow to the Kubernetes cluster using the CLI.

```bash

kfctl apply -V -f https://github.com/kubeflow/manifests/archive/v1.10.0.tar.gz

```

> Deploy Kubeflow using the specified version.

steps.title: Post-installation configuration
steps.description:
This step configures additional features like autoscaling and monitoring.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kubeflow/manifests/v1.10.0/monitoring/monitoring.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kubeflow/manifests/v1.10.0/autoscaling/autoscaling.yaml

```

> Set up monitoring for Kubeflow.
> Enable autoscaling for workloads.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Kubeflow
uninstall.description:
This step removes Kubeflow from the cluster. Be aware that this will delete all associated resources and data.

```bash

kfctl delete -V -f https://github.com/kubeflow/manifests/archive/v1.10.0.tar.gz

gcloud container clusters delete kubeflow-cluster --region=us-central1 --quiet

```

> Remove Kubeflow resources.
> Delete the GKE cluster and all orphaned resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Kubeflow
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your data. This step will upgrade your Kubeflow installation to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get all --all-namespaces > kubeflow-backup.yaml

kfctl apply -V -f https://github.com/kubeflow/manifests/archive/v1.10.0.tar.gz

kubectl get pods -n kubeflow-system

```

> Backup current resources.
> Upgrade to the latest version.
> Verify that all pods are running after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'kubectl: command not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The kubectl CLI is not installed or not in the PATH.

**Fix:**
Install kubectl by following the official installation guide.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to the server'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The kubeconfig is not set up correctly or the cluster is not running.

**Fix:**
Run 'gcloud container clusters get-credentials kubeflow-cluster --region=us-central1' to set up kubeconfig.

troubleshooting.title: Pods in 'Pending' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Insufficient resources in the cluster.

**Fix:**
Check node resources and consider scaling the cluster or adjusting resource requests.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'kubeflow not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Kubeflow has not been deployed or the namespace is incorrect.

**Fix:**
Ensure Kubeflow is deployed with 'kfctl apply -V -f <manifest-url>' and check the namespace.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new components in the AI lifecycle, improved integration with existing Kubernetes resources.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated certain legacy APIs in favor of more efficient alternatives.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v1.9, ensure to review the new component configurations and adjust your manifests accordingly.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get pods -n kubeflow-system' showing all pods in the 'Running' state.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a GCP account and the necessary permissions to create Kubernetes clusters.
